EventName: Aguila Breakaway & Calf Roping – Wednesdays & Sundays
PrizeMoney: $500 Added if 50 entries in Breakaway Open
Description: Weekly Breakaway & Calf Roping every Wednesday and Sunday at 49507 N 527th Drive, Aguila, AZ. Starts 11 AM. BREAKAWAY WARM UP: 2 for $60, 50% payback. BREAKAWAY OPEN: 3 for $150, 35% stock charge ($500 added if 50 entries), non-progressive. CALF ROPING Under 16/Over 50: 3 for $200. CALF ROPING OPEN: 3 for $250, 40% stock charge, non-progressive. Enter all ropings 3x. Cash only.
Zabawa Arena Friday weekly #8.5 team roping, Maricopa, AZ. Capped at 4+. $150 entry, 3-steer progressive, 80% payback. Cash only.
5 Flat Monday Ropings at Flying E Ranch, Wickenburg, AZ. Every Monday. 30+ and Ladies divisions. #12.5, #11.5, #10.5 handicaps. $125/roper, 4-steer PA1, enter 3x. Fresh cattle. Cash only.
Zabawa Arena Monday weekly #9.5 team roping, Maricopa, AZ. For 40+ or any age ladies, capped at 5+. $150 entry, 3-steer progressive, 80% payback. Cash only.

EventName: High Call Productions Saturday & Sunday Ropings
Time: Books open 9 AM, close 10 AM, rope 10:15 AM
Description: High Call Productions weekend team roping at Flying E Ranch, Wickenburg, AZ. Books open 9 AM, close 10 AM, rope 10:15 AM. SATURDAYS (40+ & All Ladies): #12.5 Handicap 4-steer $100/man enter 3x 80% payback; #8.5 Handicap (50+ & Ladies) 3-steer $120/man enter 3x or draw up to 6; #9.5 Handicap (50+ & Ladies) 4-steer $150/man enter 2x or draw up to 4 70% payback. SUNDAYS (All Ages): #7.5 Handicap 3-steer $120/man knives to average winners; #9.5 Handicap 3-steer $120/man; #11.5 Handicap 4-steer $150/man 70% payback. Cash only. USTRC/WSTR card required.

EventName: Arizona Cutting Horse Association - May Madness and Novice Challenge
Description: Arizona Cutting Horse Association May Madness and Novice Challenge at Horseshoe Park — five days of NCHA-sanctioned cutting including the Novice Challenge for newer horses and riders. Open, Non-Pro, Amateur and Novice divisions. Contact Kathy Unfried at 928-277-6103 or arizonacuttinghorse@gmail.com.

EventName: Turquoise Circuit PRCA Rodeo - Clovis NM
Description: Turquoise Circuit PRCA Pro Rodeo in Clovis, New Mexico. Three days of professional rodeo featuring all PRCA events. Clovis is a major stop on the New Mexico leg of the Turquoise Circuit. Visit prorodeo.com for official schedule and competitor entry details.

Pacific Ridge Show Jumping Spring 2 Regional at LAEC — three days of regional hunter/jumper competition. Designed to be more accessible and affordable than the national shows, inviting riders who can't commit to multi-week circuits. Free for spectators. Entries at prsj.showgroundslive.com.

Pinnacle Circuit Horse Show at South Point Arena in Las Vegas. Five days of premier AQHA-affiliated quarter horse competition. Free to the public. Part of the Pinnacle Circuit series leading to the AQHA West Level 1 Championships. Visit pinnaclecircuit.com for class schedule.

Horsinalities Pony Club 10-week summer session at Come Back Ranch Animal Sanctuary in Eagar. Ages 7–18. Every Friday 9 AM–1 PM. $600 for 10 weeks or $60 per session. Learn horse care, horsinalities, tie-dye crafts, horseshoe decorating and horse painting. Scholarships available. comebackranch.org.

Pacific Ridge Show Jumping Spring 3 Regional at LAEC — three days of regional hunter/jumper competition at the Los Angeles Equestrian Center in Burbank. Part of the 10-event 2026 Pacific Ridge series spanning April through October. Free for spectators. Entries at prsj.showgroundslive.com.

EventName: Huachuca Saddle Club Gymkhana Series 1 - May 9
Description: Huachuca Saddle Club Gymkhana Series at The Outlaw Arena in Hereford. Barrels, poles, and two additional classes across six age divisions from lead line to 40 & over. Adult $9/class, Youth $8/class, or $40 flat for members. Must ride 3 of 4 dates to qualify for awards.

EventName: Central Arizona Riding Academy (CARA) Schooling Show Series
Description: CARA's May schooling show is a one-day dressage test opportunity focused on good mileage and constructive judging in a relaxed environment. Great for riders prepping for summer and fall recognized shows, introducing horses to the show ring, or simply putting a test on paper. Registration details are posted on CARA's website.

AQHA West Level 1 Championships at South Point Arena in Las Vegas. Six days of American Quarter Horse Association championship competition for Level 1 competitors from the western region. Free to the public. Visit aqha.com for class schedule and qualifying info.

Texas country music favorites Randy Rogers Band bring their live show to The Maverick in Tucson. Known for hits like "Kiss Me in the Dark," "In My Arms Instead," and "Trouble," the band has been a staple of the Texas/Red Dirt country scene for over two decades. Their energetic live performances and authentic country sound make them a must-see for fans of real country music. The Maverick provides an intimate venue setting perfect for experiencing this acclaimed band up close.

Turquoise Circuit PRCA Pro Rodeo in Payson, AZ — spring edition. Two days of professional rodeo in the Rim Country featuring all PRCA events. Payson hosts two circuit rodeos per year, spring and late summer. Visit paysonprorodeo.com for tickets and schedule details.

Three day show jumping competition hosted by Pinnacle Equestrian Tour. Covered arena with premium stalls with mats, VIP options, full bar, and electric evening classes under the lights. RV spots and stall reservations available. Entries open on Showgrounds Live. Restaurant and veterinary on site.

EventName: Crown B Ranch 3rd Annual Masters Team Roping
Time: Friday: books open 6 PM, rope 7 PM. Saturday: books open 8 AM, rope 9 AM.
PrizeMoney: 80% payback, trophy letterman jackets, saddles to average winners
Description: Crown B Ranch 3rd Annual Masters Team Roping, May 15–16, 2026 at Pima County Fairgrounds, Tucson, AZ. Produced by Savantos Land & Cattle and Crown B Ranch LLC. FRIDAY WARM-UP (books open 6 PM, rope 7 PM): #13.5 Hailstorm Roofing Slide – pick or draw $100, enter 4x, 4-steer average, 80% payback, trophy letterman jackets. Junior 12 & Under Tractor Supply Dummy Roping 6 PM. SATURDAY (books open 8 AM, rope 9 AM): #11.5 Saddle Roping – P1/D1 or Draw 2 $150, enter 3x, 4-steer average, no cap, buckles to average winners 2nd–4th. #9.5 Saddle Roping – P1/D1 or Draw 2 $150, enter 3x, 3-steer average, no cap. All ropings 2/3 cash prize payback unless otherwise noted. Must have current USTRC, WS, or NTR card.

EventName: BR Diamond Spur Barrels & Poles Series - May 16
Description: BR Diamond Spur Barrels & Poles Series at Charles Whitlow Rodeo Arena. 80% payback, $500 added, cash only. Barrels: Open 5D $50, Youth 3D $40, Rookie 3D $20. Poles: 4D $50. Must enter 3 of 4 jackpots to qualify for awards.

North American Trail Ride Conference (NATRC) Region 2 LeD Rides presents 'Showdown at Sunset' at Sunset Ranch. Sunset Ranch Productions. Fabulous location, beautiful area, fun trails. Ride Manager: Kandace French Contreras.

Scottsdale Saddle Club gymkhana at the Rodeo Grounds featuring professional grade ground and facilities with divisions for every level of rider. Part of the 2026 season series running monthly through December. Visit scottsdalesaddleclub.com for full details, classes, and entry information.

Showdown at Sunset NATRC Ride — Where Legends are Made. Leisure Division competitive trail ride at the historic Route 66 rodeo grounds in Seligman, AZ. Ride one or two days. Open to Adult and Junior divisions. Hosted by Kandace French Contreras.

APHA Zone 2 Horse Show at South Point Arena in Las Vegas. Four days of American Paint Horse Association zone-level competition open to all APHA members. Free to the public. Visit aphazone2.com for class schedule, entry deadlines and hotel information.

One of NRCHA's premier major events for 4- and 5-year-old cow horses, offering multiple divisions from Open and Non Pro to boxing and limited classes. The Derby features snaffle and hackamore competition, special spectacles (including bridle events), and a Run For A Million qualifier. Two weeks of shows, vendors, and championship finals at WestWorld.

NBHA Las Vegas Super Show at South Point Arena — one of the biggest barrel racing events of the year. Four days of National Barrel Horse Association competition with Open, Youth and amateur divisions. Free to the public. Visit nbha.com for entry info and class schedule.

Tri-Lakes CHA hosts the Ozark Circuit's NCHA Days event in Lakeview, Arkansas. NCHA Days is a circuit program spotlight that delivers double circuit points, plus added money and awards, in an exclusive May show window. It's an ideal weekend for riders and owners looking to make a meaningful jump in circuit standings with a single focused event.

EventName: Turquoise Circuit PRCA Rodeo - Window Rock Navajo Nation Fair
Description: Turquoise Circuit PRCA Pro Rodeo at the Navajo Nation Fairgrounds in Window Rock, AZ. Three days of professional rodeo honoring the rich Native American rodeo tradition. Features all PRCA events with strong Native American cowboy participation. Visit nnfair.org for schedule and tickets.

EventName: Three Day Clinic with Carl Bledsoe
Description: Three day horsemanship clinic with Carl Bledsoe, 2022 Humane Horseman of the Year. Focus on classical horsemanship training for horses and riders of all disciplines, specializing in gaited horse breeds. Topics include gait analysis, biomechanics and horse anatomy, groundwork and communication techniques, and rider balance and harmony. Clinic fee $850.

EventName: Chino Valley Cowboy Challenge and EXCA Race Series - May 30-31
Description: High Plain Tumblers present the Chino Valley Cowboy Challenge and EXCA Race Series. Saturday: workshops 9 AM–1 PM plus Cowboy Challenge obstacle course. Sunday: EXCA Sanctioned Double Point races with divisions from Young Guns to Pro. Judges Dee Ladd and Jeff Cook. Overnight stalls available. cvcowboychallenge@gmail.com.

Turquoise Circuit PRCA Pro Rodeo in Flagstaff, AZ. Three days of professional rodeo at 7,000 feet elevation in the pines. Features all PRCA events including bull riding, barrel racing and team roping. A cool summer escape with great rodeo action. Visit flagstaffrodeo.com for tickets.

Wild West Saddle Up 2026 at Buckin' Burro Ranch, Prescott. Weekend Pass $325 includes guided trail rides, 4 meals, ranch activities and Saturday Shindig. Shindig-Only $125 for Saturday night live music, cocktails, dinner and auction. Music by Palomino Country. Benefits Animal Guardian Network sanctuary. Contact Carrie 602-568-5636.

PCCHA Reyzin The Cash Derby at South Point Arena in Las Vegas. Ten days of Pacific Coast Cutting Horse Association competition featuring the top cutting horses and riders on the West Coast. Free to the public. Visit pccha.com for entry information and full class schedule.

Pacific Ridge Show Jumping Summer 1 at LAEC — part of the 10-event 2026 series spanning April through October at the Los Angeles Equestrian Center in Burbank. National-level hunter/jumper competition with new footing upgrades. Free for spectators. Entries at prsj.showgroundslive.com.

EventName: Working Equitation Clinic with Tessa Nicolet
Description: Working equitation clinic and private lessons with Tessa Nicolet, USAWE Professional Instructor and L Judge. Full day clinic covers dressage and obstacle handling. June 7 offers custom private lessons. New and experienced riders welcome. Camping and pens available. Sign up at CohesiveHorsemanship.com.

EventName: Obstacle Clinic and Challenge - Dos Lomas
Description: Obstacle Clinic and Challenge at Dos Lomas Wild Ranch in Snowflake — over 30 obstacles in the White Mountains. Clinic 8–10 AM, open practice 10 AM–2 PM, challenge from 2 PM with awards. $75/horse-rider combo includes all. Gift for every entry, prizes for top 5. Contact: dlwildranch@gmail.com.

EventName: Working Equitation Clinic and Private Lessons with Tessa Nicolet
Description: Working Equitation clinic and private lessons with Tessa Nicolet, USAWE Professional Instructor and L judge. June 6 full day WE Clinic starts with dressage then applies dressage work to obstacles for ease of handling trial. June 7 private lessons fully custom to individual needs. New and experienced riders welcome. Camping and pens available.

EventName: 20th Annual FUNdamentals Horse Camp & FUNkhana & Trail Challenge
Description: 20th Annual FUNdamentals Horse Camp at Buckin Burro Ranch near Prescott. Five days and four nights of horsemanship for girls ages 9-16. Includes meals, housing, stalls, individualized instruction, speed events, trail rides, daily challenges, award ceremony, and trophys. Indoor arena and trails. Limited to 24 spots. $200 deposit to reserve.

Venue: Stargazer Pavilion - Cliff Castle Casino Hotel
Description: Bull Bash Championship Bullriding returns to Stargazer Pavilion at Cliff Castle Casino in Camp Verde. Open $3,000 added/$100 entry, Novice $500 added/$75 entry, both 100% payback. Mutton Bustin' 6 & under $20 at 6:30 PM. Entries open June 1–5. Text 928-821-0489 to enter.

Arizona Dressage Association's Summer Heat recognized show offers USDF/USEF qualifying-score opportunities in a professionally managed setting at WestWorld of Scottsdale. Open to multiple levels and divisions, including Para Dressage and Western Dressage, with scheduling designed to avoid peak heat. Ideal for riders targeting championships and year-end awards.

EventName: ADA Some More Summer Heat Recognized Show
Description: Back-to-back with Summer Heat, ADA's Some More Summer Heat recognized show provides another full day to earn USDF/USEF qualifying scores at WestWorld of Scottsdale. Competitors can maximize travel by riding both days, with divisions spanning Intro through Grand Prix plus Para and Western Dressage options. A strong mid-summer checkpoint for fall goals.

EventName: Gold Rush Classic Peruvian Horse Show
Description: Gold Rush Classic Peruvian Horse Show at South Point Arena in Las Vegas. Three days of Southern California Peruvian Paso Horse Club competition showcasing the elegance and smooth gait of Peruvian horses. Free to the public. Visit scpphc.org for class schedule and entry details.

EventName: Turquoise Circuit PRCA Rodeo - El Paso TX
Description: Turquoise Circuit PRCA Pro Rodeo in El Paso, TX. Three days of professional rodeo on the Texas-New Mexico border. Features all PRCA events with strong participation from Texas, New Mexico and Arizona cowboys. Visit hiloprorodeo.com for tickets and competitor information.

The Celebration at South Point Arena in Las Vegas — eleven days of premier NSBA and EMO western performance horse competition. One of the largest western horse shows in the country featuring ranch riding, reining, cow horse and more. Free to the public. Visit thecelebrationlasvegas.com.

EventName: Turquoise Circuit PRCA Rodeo - Santa Fe NM
Description: Rodeo de Santa Fe — Turquoise Circuit PRCA Pro Rodeo in Santa Fe, NM. Three days of professional rodeo in the historic City of Holy Faith. Features all PRCA events. One of the most scenic rodeo settings in the Southwest combining world-class competition with New Mexico culture. Visit rodeodesantafe.org.

EventName: Central Arizona Riding Academy (CARA) Schooling Show Series
Description: CARA's late-June schooling show is a one-day dressage test event built for practical show experience and helpful judge feedback. A good option for riders wanting to keep horses in a consistent competition routine through the summer. Visit CARA online for entries, schedules, and any division updates.

EventName: Turquoise Circuit PRCA Rodeo - Prescott World's Oldest Rodeo
Description: Prescott Frontier Days — the World's Oldest Rodeo, a Turquoise Circuit PRCA event since 1888. Seven days of July 4th professional rodeo featuring all PRCA events. One of Arizona's premier western celebrations combining rodeo, parade and community festivities. Visit worldsoldestrodeo.com.

Two day clinic with Steve Kutie, national champion in multiple events and international competitor. $600 per rider for both days. $10 haul-in fee per horse per day for facility use. $20 audit fee. Hosted by Yeguada Karma CH Traditional PRE Andalusians and Nathan Burger at Santori Ranch, a full service boarding and training stable family owned since 1994.

Pacific Ridge Show Jumping Summer 2 at LAEC — part of the 10-event 2026 series spanning April through October at the Los Angeles Equestrian Center in Burbank. Regional-level hunter/jumper competition with new footing upgrades. Free for spectators. Entries at prsj.showgroundslive.com.

EventName: Morgan Medallion Classic Charity Horse Show
Description: Morgan Medallion Classic Charity Horse Show at South Point's Priefert Pavilion in Las Vegas. Five days of Morgan horse competition raising funds for charity. Free to the public. A showcase of the Morgan breed's versatility and athleticism. Visit morganmedallion.com for entry and schedule info.

NACADA's July schooling show at Freeman Farms offers classical and western dressage tests in a welcoming, educational setting. Designed for riders seeking mileage, feedback, and a relaxed competition day in Northern Arizona. Entries and show specifics are posted on NACADA's calendar and entry page.

CMSA Western US Championship at South Point Arena in Las Vegas. Four days of Cowboy Mounted Shooting Association western regional championship competition. Riders in period costume shoot balloon targets at full gallop. Free to the public. Visit cmsaevents.com for division and entry info.

EventName: Turquoise Circuit PRCA Rodeo - Lovington NM Lea County Fair
Description: Turquoise Circuit PRCA Pro Rodeo at the Lea County Fair in Lovington, NM. Nine days of professional rodeo and fair activities in southeastern New Mexico. Features all PRCA events. One of the longer-running circuit events on the New Mexico leg of the Turquoise Circuit. Visit leacountyfair.net.

Pacific Ridge Show Jumping Summer 3 at LAEC — part of the 10-event 2026 series spanning April through October at the Los Angeles Equestrian Center in Burbank. National-level hunter/jumper competition with new footing upgrades. Free for spectators. Entries at prsj.showgroundslive.com.

The Run For A Million at South Point Arena — the richest reining event in the world with a $1 million purse. Features Reining, Cutting Horse Challenge, Cow Horse Challenge, Youth and Non-Pro events. Free general admission with ticketed championship nights. Visit therunforamillion.com.

EventName: National Stock Horse Association Futurity and Derby
Description: National Stock Horse Association Futurity and Derby at South Point Arena in Las Vegas. Six days of NSHA competition showcasing top stock horses in ranch riding, reining and cow horse events. Free to the public. Visit nationalstockhorse.com for class schedule and entry details.

EventName: Will Rogers Polo Club - Equidome Women's Challenge
Description: Will Rogers Polo Club Equidome Women's Challenge arena polo tournament at LAEC. Two days of women's arena polo competition inside the iconic Equidome. Free for spectators. An exciting and accessible introduction to the sport of polo for Los Angeles fans. Visit thelaec.com for details.

EventName: Turquoise Circuit PRCA Rodeo - Payson (Late Summer)
Description: Turquoise Circuit PRCA Pro Rodeo in Payson, AZ — late summer edition. Two days of professional rodeo in the cool Arizona Rim Country. Payson hosts two Turquoise Circuit rodeos per year. Features all PRCA events. Visit paysonprorodeo.com for tickets, schedule and competitor information.

EventName: Turquoise Circuit PRCA Rodeo - Socorro NM County Fair
Description: Turquoise Circuit PRCA Pro Rodeo at the Socorro County Fair in Socorro, NM. Two days of professional rodeo and county fair events in central New Mexico. Features all PRCA events. Visit socorronm.gov for schedule and event details.

Turquoise Circuit PRCA Pro Rodeo in Scottsdale, AZ — fall edition. Date TBA. Professional rodeo featuring all PRCA events at WestWorld of Scottsdale. One of two Scottsdale stops on the Turquoise Circuit. Visit rodeoscottsdale.com for confirmed dates and ticket information.

Buckles and Barrels for Bailey at South Point Arena in Las Vegas. Three days of barrel racing competition and celebration raising funds for a meaningful cause. Free to the public. Visit bucklesandbarrels4bailey.com for entry information, divisions and schedule.

September NACADA schooling show at Freeman Farms in Chino Valley features classical and western dressage tests with a supportive show atmosphere. A strong tune-up before fall championships and recognized shows, with an emphasis on learning and good horsemanship. See NACADA's entry page for the latest details and forms.

EventName: High Roller Reining Classic Horse Show
Description: High Roller Reining Classic at South Point Arena in Las Vegas. Eight days of premier NRHA-affiliated reining competition. One of the most prestigious reining events of the fall season with Open, Non-Pro, Amateur and Youth divisions. Free to the public. Visit highrollerreiningclassic.com.

EventName: Turquoise Circuit PRCA Rodeo - New Mexico State Fair Albuquerque
Description: Turquoise Circuit PRCA Pro Rodeo at the New Mexico State Fair in Albuquerque. Eleven days of professional rodeo at one of the largest state fairs in the Southwest. Features all PRCA events. A major Turquoise Circuit points event drawing top competitors from AZ and NM. Visit statefair.exponm.com.

Pacific Ridge Show Jumping Fall 1 at LAEC — part of the 10-event 2026 series spanning April through October at the Los Angeles Equestrian Center in Burbank. Regional-level hunter/jumper competition with new footing upgrades. Free for spectators. Entries at prsj.showgroundslive.com.

Save the date for the Rio Verde Roverettes All Woman's Trail Ride 2026! This year's theme celebrates America's Birthday - 250 Years. The multi-day trail ride takes place in the beautiful Coconino National Forest, offering scenic riding through Arizona's high country. This popular annual event brings together women riders for camaraderie, adventure, and appreciation of the outdoors on horseback. Invitations and detailed information will be emailed out June 1. Perfect for experienced trail riders looking for a memorable group riding experience in one of Arizona's most scenic forests.

Scottsdale Saddle Club gymkhana at the Rodeo Grounds featuring professional grade ground and facilities with divisions for every level of rider. Part of the 2026 season series running monthly through December. Visit scottsdalesaddleclub.com for full details, classes, and entry information.

EventName: PCCHA Holy Cow Performance Horses and Super Futurity
Description: PCCHA Holy Cow Performance Horses and Super Futurity at South Point Arena in Las Vegas. Six days of Pacific Coast Cutting Horse Association futurity competition featuring young horses and top trainers competing for major prizes. Free to the public. Visit pccha.com for full schedule.

Scottsdale Saddle Club gymkhana at the Rodeo Grounds featuring professional grade ground and facilities with divisions for every level of rider. Part of the 2026 season series running monthly through December. Visit scottsdalesaddleclub.com for full details, classes, and entry information.

Arizona Cutting Horse Association Oktoberfest at Horseshoe Park — six days of fall NCHA-sanctioned cutting horse competition. Open, Non-Pro, Amateur, Limited and Youth divisions. One of AZCHA's premier fall events kicking off the new season. Contact Kathy Unfried 928-277-6103 or arizonacuttinghorse@gmail.com.

Pacific Ridge Show Jumping Fall 2 (Finals) at LAEC — part of the 10-event 2026 series spanning April through October at the Los Angeles Equestrian Center in Burbank. National-level hunter/jumper competition with new footing upgrades. Free for spectators. Entries at prsj.showgroundslive.com.

EventName: K/A Productions - SCHSA Hunter Derby Finals
Description: K/A Productions SCHSA Hunter Derby Finals at LAEC — featuring the $15,000 SCHSA Hunter Derby Finals, jumper classics and costume classes. Three days of competition with year-end division champions. Free for spectators. Visit ka-productions.com.

APHA Western National Championship at South Point Arena in Las Vegas. Four days of American Paint Horse Association national-level competition for western performance, halter and pleasure horses. Free to the public. Visit apha.com for class schedule, entry info and hotel group rates.

Pacific Ridge Show Jumping Fall 3 at LAEC — part of the 10-event 2026 series spanning April through October at the Los Angeles Equestrian Center in Burbank. Regional-level hunter/jumper competition with new footing upgrades. Free for spectators. Entries at prsj.showgroundslive.com.

The Desert Crown Youth Rodeo at Buckeye Equestrian and Events Center. Presented by Paloma Ready Mix and Materials. Arizona High School Rodeo Association sanctioned event featuring youth rodeo competition over three days. A premier youth rodeo in the Phoenix metro area.

EventName: American Bucking Bull Inc World Finals
Description: American Bucking Bull Inc (ABBI) World Finals at South Point Arena in Las Vegas. Six days of championship bull riding and bucking bull competition with the world's top bulls and riders. Free to the public. Visit americanbuckingbull.com for full schedule and competition details.

Venue: Cave Creek Memorial Arena Rodeo Grounds
Description: Turquoise Circuit Finals Rodeo in Cave Creek — the championship event for the PRCA Turquoise Circuit covering Arizona and New Mexico. Top two qualifiers from each event advance to the RAM National Circuit Finals. Three nights of elite rodeo with VIP Turquoise Trail Lounge and High Desert Sky Box options. Tickets at turquoisecircuitfinalsrodeo.com.

EventName: ADA Fall Fiesta (Double Show) & Arizona State Dressage Championships
Description: ADA's Fall Fiesta is a two-day USDF/USEF recognized double show that also hosts the Arizona State Dressage Championships at WestWorld of Scottsdale. Riders compete for qualifying scores, year-end recognition, and state championship titles across multiple levels and divisions, including Para and Western Dressage. A marquee fall destination show for Arizona dressage.

Marshall+Sterling/USHJA National Championships at South Point Arena in Las Vegas. Seven days of US Hunter Jumper Association national championship competition for hunters, jumpers and equitation riders of all levels. Free to the public. Visit ushja.org for class schedule and qualifying requirements.

CINCH All Star Super Sort at South Point's Priefert Pavilion in Las Vegas. Three days of premier ranch sorting competition sponsored by CINCH. Open to competitors of all skill levels. Free to the public. Visit rsnc.us for entry information, divisions and full schedule.

Scottsdale Saddle Club gymkhana at the Rodeo Grounds featuring professional grade ground and facilities with divisions for every level of rider. Part of the 2026 season series running monthly through December. Visit scottsdalesaddleclub.com for full details, classes, and entry information.

The Cup Series presents The Turkey Cup barrel race at Dillon's Western Trails Arena. In conjunction with Dillon's Thanksgiving Day Race. Entries open Nov 1. $600 entry fee, limited to 200 entries. Projected payout with 80% back and 200 entries: 1st $6,400 & Trophy Cup, 2nd $5,760, 3rd $4,800, 4th $4,000, 5th $3,200, 6th $2,560, 7th $1,920, 8th $1,280, 9th $1,120, 10th $960. Times can be carried over from each run throughout the race. Trophy cups to each D winner. 3D equal payouts on 0.6 splits.

Scottsdale Saddle Club gymkhana at the Rodeo Grounds featuring professional grade ground and facilities with divisions for every level of rider. Part of the 2026 season series running monthly through December. Visit scottsdalesaddleclub.com for full details, classes, and entry information.

Year-end Dressage at Spear-Cross recognized Level 2 show in Scottsdale. Two days of competition with licensed judging and a professional show setup at Spear-Cross Equine Center. A strong December option for riders targeting late-season qualifying scores and championship preparation. Check the event page for specifics and updates.

Silver Dollar Circuit returns to fabulous Las Vegas January 12–16, 2027 with new dates. A premier destination horse show event drawing competitors from across the southwest. Plan ahead for this multi-day circuit show in Las Vegas, Nevada.
#8.5 roping capped at 4+. $150 entry, 3-steer progressive, 80% payback. Cash only.
30+ and Ladies. #12.5, #11.5, #10.5 divisions. $125/roper, 4-steer PA1, enter 3x. Fresh cattle. Cash only.
#9.5 roping for 40+ or any age ladies, capped at 5+. $150 entry, 3-steer progressive, 80% payback. Cash only.

EventName: Team Roping Lessons with Cesar de la Cruz
Description: Train with 9-time NFR heeler Cesar de la Cruz in Casa Grande, Arizona. With over $1.3 million in lifetime earnings and three PRCA Tour Finale titles, Cesar brings elite-level experience to every lesson. Private and semi-private sessions are available for ropers of all skill levels looking to improve their technique, timing, and competitive edge. Whether you're working on your swing, positioning, or horse handling, Cesar provides personalized coaching tailored to your goals. His philosophy focuses on giving students all the tools for success in the arena. Contact via social media using #RopeWithCesar to schedule your session and take your heeling to the next level.

Time: Books Close at 10:30 AM, Rope at 11:00 AM
Description: Open Breakaway every Saturday. 3 Head Progressive. Bell Collar Catch Only. Starts with the Open. March 14th will be $200/run in the Open. Cash Only. All ropings are 2/3rd payback unless specified. Ropings nominated for WCRA. Sponsored by Roping Finder & Akins Trailer Sales.

4D Arena Breakaway every Saturday! Books close at 10:30am, rope at 11:00am. Starts with the open. OPEN BREAKAWAY: $120 entry, Enter 4x max, 3 head progressive, bell collar catch only. NOVICE BREAKAWAY: $120 entry, Enter 4x max, straight 2 head average. Novice eligibility - must meet ONE: Woman over 50, kid under 12, or roper/horse with less than $1500 lifetime earnings. Cash only! All ropings are 2/3rd payback unless specified. Ropings are nominated for WCRA. Sponsored by Roping Finder and Akins Trailer Sales. Follow Hot Shot Equine and 4D Arena on Facebook for updates.

High Call Productions offers weekend team roping at Flying E Ranch. Books open 9am, close 10am, rope at 10:15am. SATURDAYS (Over 40 & All Ladies): #12.5 Handicap - 4-steer $100/man, enter 3x, 80% payback. #8.5 Handicap (50+ & Ladies) - 3-steer $120/man, enter 3x or draw up to 6. #9.5 Handicap (50+ & Ladies) - 4-steer $150/man, enter 2x or draw up to 4, 70% payback. SUNDAYS (All Ages): #7.5 Handicap - 3-steer $120/man, knives to average winners. #9.5 Handicap - 3-steer $120/man. #11.5 Handicap - 4-steer $150/man, 70% payback. Cash only. USTRC/WSTR card required.
Weekly Sunday ropings. #7.5 over 40/ladies $100/man, #8.5 $120/man, #9.5 Handicap $120/man. Trophy buckles.

High Call Productions offers weekend team roping at Flying E Ranch. Books open 9am, close 10am, rope at 10:15am. SATURDAYS (Over 40 & All Ladies): #12.5 Handicap - 4-steer $100/man, enter 3x, 80% payback. #8.5 Handicap (50+ & Ladies) - 3-steer $120/man, enter 3x or draw up to 6. #9.5 Handicap (50+ & Ladies) - 4-steer $150/man, enter 2x or draw up to 4, 70% payback. SUNDAYS (All Ages): #7.5 Handicap - 3-steer $120/man, knives to average winners. #9.5 Handicap - 3-steer $120/man. #11.5 Handicap - 4-steer $150/man, 70% payback. Cash only. USTRC/WSTR card required.

Western Trails "The Bar" offers weekly Heel-O-Matic practice sessions every Tuesday in Morristown. The morning begins with Heel-O-Matic Dummy Practice from 10:00-11:00 AM ($20/horse), followed by Live Cattle Practice from 11:00 AM-1:00 PM ($40/horse, $25 additional horses). This is an ideal opportunity for ropers to work on their timing and technique in a low-pressure environment with quality cattle. Whether you're tuning up your heel horse or developing your skills, Western Trails provides a professional practice setting with experienced management.
#12.5 Slide Tuesday Night. $200 entry, 4-steer progressive handicapped, 80% payback. Cash only.

EventName: Tom Wicklund Drills & Maneuvers - Weekly Classes
Description: Weekly group horsemanship classes with Tom Wicklund at Performance Horse Arena. Tuesdays and Wednesdays at 5:30 PM, Thursdays at 10 AM. Focus on balance, hind quarters, forehand, and flexing. All disciplines and breeds welcome. New topics each week. $30 per session, space limited, pre-register.
Weekly afternoon/evening ropings. #11.5 Handicap $150/man, #12.5 Slide $150/man. 4-head progressive.
Weekly morning ropings. #6.5 $50/man, #7.5 $60/man, #8.5 $60/man. All 2-steer with trophy buckles. Wrangler certificates to high money.

EventName: Twisted N Productions Wednesday Round Robin
Venue: Bottoms Up Horse Arena & RV Park
Description: Twisted N Productions weekly Wednesday round robin at Bottoms Up Horse Arena in Casa Grande. $200/man, 15 headers and 15 heelers. Starts at 6 PM. Call to enter. A great mid-week roping opportunity in the Casa Grande area. Call/text 602-541-8880 for entries.

EventName: Sorta Sorting Practice & Ranch Horse Activities
Description: Weekly sorta sorting practice plus obstacle course and trail course at Flying E Ranch's new arena in Wickenburg. Wednesdays and Saturdays starting at 9 AM. Presented by Wickenburg's Ultimate Ranch & Stock Horse Challenge. Open to all levels looking to sharpen their skills.

EventName: Thirsty Thursday Open Mic and Bull Riding Practice
Description: Weekly Thirsty Thursday night at Roadrunner Restaurant and Saloon on Black Canyon Hwy. Open Mic 6–9 PM with live music talent. Live Bull Riding Practice starts at 7 PM. A unique Arizona combo of country music and roughstock action every Thursday night.

Chandler Vaqueros Saddle Club monthly rodeos at Horseshoe Park — season runs September through May. Events include Goat Pulling, Goat Tying, Barrel Racing, Pole Bending and Speed events. Age groups 3–8, 9–12, 13–18, 19–39 and 40+. Free for spectators. All levels welcome, volunteer-run nonprofit club.

QC 4H Horse Project monthly meetings at Horseshoe Park in Queen Creek. Youth horse program focused on horsemanship, animal husbandry and community leadership. Open to youth ages 5–18 interested in horses and equestrian activities. Contact Horseshoe Park at 480-358-3793 for meeting schedule.

Heart Cry Cowboy Church monthly gathering at Horseshoe Park in Queen Creek. A community worship experience in the western tradition welcoming horses, families and all who appreciate the cowboy way of life. Free to attend. Check Horseshoe Park event calendar for specific dates.

Description: The #7.5 2-Steer Cash Roping is the 1st roping of the 7th Annual Memorial Day Cash Explosion in Gilbert, Arizona on Saturday May 23rd. $20,000 to the Hi Point Winner (guaranteed at 300 paid teams) plus $30K in total Hi Point money across both ropings.
